tagline: Now in Public Preview for external Iceberg compatibility.
what_it_does: Databricks now supports the creation of materialized views that are compatible with external Iceberg readers, allowing users to leverage Iceberg's capabilities for analytics workloads. This feature enables users to create materialized views using the `USING ICEBERG` syntax, which can be read by external systems but not written to. The materialized views are managed by Unity Catalog, ensuring lifecycle management and integration with other Databricks features.
why_it_matters: This update enhances interoperability with external systems, allowing organizations to utilize Iceberg's advanced features while maintaining data integrity and performance. It benefits data engineers and analysts by providing a seamless way to access and analyze data across different platforms, ultimately improving productivity and decision-making processes.
plain_summary: Databricks now supports the creation of materialized views that are compatible with external Iceberg readers, allowing users to leverage Iceberg's capabilities for analytics workloads. This feature enables users to create materialized views using the USING…

capabilities.title: External Compatibility
capabilities.desc: Materialized views can be read by external Iceberg readers, enhancing data accessibility.

capabilities.title: Unity Catalog Management
capabilities.desc: Lifecycle tasks for materialized views are managed by Unity Catalog, ensuring efficient data governance.

capabilities.title: Incremental Refresh Support
capabilities.desc: Materialized views support incremental refresh, keeping results up to date as source data changes.

capabilities.title: SQL Syntax Integration
capabilities.desc: Users can create materialized views using standard SQL syntax with `USING ICEBERG`.

use_cases: Data analysis using external Iceberg engines.
Integration of Iceberg materialized views in multi-platform data workflows.
Real-time analytics with up-to-date materialized views.
limitations: External Iceberg readers can only read materialized views; they cannot write to them.
Materialized views are subject to the limitations of managed Iceberg tables.

steps.title: Enable Feature
steps.desc: Contact your Databricks account team to enable managed Iceberg materialized views.

steps.title: Create Materialized View
steps.desc: Use the `CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW <mv_name> USING ICEBERG AS SELECT ...` syntax.

steps.title: Repair Table
steps.desc: Run `REPAIR TABLE <mv_name> SYNC METADATA` after creation.

steps.title: Access from External Readers
steps.desc: Configure external systems to read the materialized view using the Iceberg REST Catalog API.

key_takeaway: Managed Iceberg materialized views in Public Preview enhance data accessibility and interoperability with external systems.
